description.en-US:
Distance: 12km approach (winter), 2 km approach (summer shuttle)
Elevation Gain: ~400m
Capacity: Sleeps 24 guests

Located in Yoho National Park near Lake O’Hara, Elizabeth Parker Hut is one of the ACC’s most popular huts, with a summer lottery in place to manage bookings. The hut is open year-round, with winter access requiring avalanche safety training. There are two cabins on site – the original Wiwaxy cabin and the newer log cabin – both available on a first-come, first-served basis for booked guests. This hut offers a cozy alpine base for hikers in summer and ski tourers in winter.

Distance: 11km approach
Elevation Gain: 520m
Capacity: Sleeps 22 guests

Located near Takkakaw Falls in Yoho National Park, the Little Yoho Valley (Stanley Mitchell) Hut is open year-round. In summer, it offers easier access along well-maintained trails, making it a great destination for families. In winter, the approach is longer and requires avalanche safety training. Sleeping up to 22 guests, this historic hut provides a comfortable base for exploring the stunning Little Yoho Valley in all seasons.

Distance: 5km scramble
Elevation Gain: 924m
Capacity: Sleeps 6 guests

Perched on the side of Castle Mountain at Castle Junction in Banff National Park, the Castle Mountain (Currie) Cabin is a summer-only climbing hut. Access requires climbing skills and comfort with exposed scrambling. This cozy cabin accommodates up to 6 guests and offers stunning views of surrounding peaks – an ideal base for alpine and rock climbing adventures.

Distance: 8km approach
Elevation Gain: 400m
Capacity: Sleeps 30 guests

Located at Bow Lake in Banff National Park, Bow Hut is open year-round. In summer, it offers hikers a stunning hub at the toe of the Wapta Glacier, while in winter it serves as a key base for adventures on the Wapta Traverse and the Bow Yoho Traverse. Winter access requires avalanche skills training.

Distance: 200m approach
Elevation Gain: 10m
Capacity: Sleeps 30 guests

Located in the Illecillewaet Campgrounds of Glacier National Park, the A.O. Wheeler Hut is open year-round. With its easy access, it offers summer visitors a great introduction to the hut experience and serves as a popular winter base for ski touring in the Asulkan Valley. This spacious hut accommodates up to 30 guests, making it ideal for both group trips and individual adventurers.

Distance: 19km approach
Elevation Gain: 320m
Capacity: Sleeps 26 guests

Located in the beautiful Tonquin Valley, this summer-only hut offers hikers a comfortable hub to explore the stunning surroundings. Accessed via a 19km trail from the Edith Cavell parking lot, the Wates-Gibson Memorial Hut sleeps up to 26 guests and provides an ideal base to experience the dramatic peaks and serene lakes of Jasper National Park.

Distance: 4.6km approach
Elevation Gain: 700m
Capacity: Sleeps 35 guests

Located in Bugaboo Provincial Park, BC, the Conrad Kain Hut is a three-level summer-only hut that sleeps up to 35 guests. This comfortable and well-equipped hut is popular with both families and climbers looking to explore the stunning granite spires surrounding the area. With Applebee Campground and Boulder Campground nearby, there is always a lively community of adventurers enjoying the breathtaking Bugaboos.

Distance: 10km approach
Elevation Gain: 670m
Capacity: Sleeps 18 guests

Located on the Wapta Glacier, this hut requires glacier travel skills and avalanche safety training to access. Starting from Peyto Lake, the 10 km approach leads to a remote alpine base sleeping up to 18 guests. The Peyto (Peter & Catharine Whyte) Hut is the first hut on the classic Wapta Traverse, offering winter adventurers a comfortable starting point for their glacier journey.

Distance: 7km approach (from Bow Lake)
Elevation Gain: 580m
Capacity: Sleeps 18 guests

Located on the Wapta Traverse, the Balfour (Rob Ritchie) Hut is the third hut along this classic glacier route. Open year-round, it requires glacier travel skills and avalanche safety training to access. Sleeping up to 18 guests, the hut offers a critical and comfortable shelter for ski tourers and mountaineers crossing the Wapta Icefield.

Access: Helicopter or unmaintained trail
Capacity: Sleeps 20 guests

Located in the stunning Selkirk Mountains, the Fairy Meadow (Bill Putnam) Hut is renowned for its annual winter ski week lottery. The hut sleeps up to 20 guests and is available for winter bookings exclusively through this lottery process. Open year-round, it can also be accessed in summer via helicopter or a challenging unmaintained trail, offering a remote alpine experience for both ski tourers and mountaineers.

Access: Helicopter only
Capacity: Sleeps 6 guests

Located in the remote Selkirk Mountains, the Great Cairn (Ben Ferris) Hut is accessible only by helicopter. Open year-round, this small hut sleeps up to 6 guests and is primarily used by mountaineers seeking solitude and challenging alpine objectives in a truly wild setting.

Distance: 11km Summer | 23km Winter
Elevation Gain:765 m
Capacity: Sleeps 12 guests

Located in Jasper National Park, the Fryatt Creek (Sydney Vallance) Hut offers hikers and climbers a comfortable base in the stunning Fryatt Valley. The hut is accessed via an 11 km hike from Brussels Campground, with a moderate elevation gain. Sleeping up to 12 guests, it is open year-round and is popular for summer alpine objectives as well as winter ski touring, though winter access requires advanced skills and preparation.

Distance: 11km approach
Elevation Gain: 1320m
Capacity: Sleeps 6 guests

Located on the east face of Mount Alberta in Jasper National Park, this summer-only hut is intended for experienced mountaineers and climbers. Access involves a rugged 11 km scramble with significant elevation gain. Sleeping a cozy 6 guests, the Mt. Alberta (Lloyd MacKay) Hut offers a remote base for challenging alpine objectives.

Distance: 26km approach (over glacier)
Elevation Gain: 610m
Capacity: Sleeps 12 guests

Located at the southern end of the Wapta Traverse, the Scott Duncan Hut serves as the final stop on this classic glacier route. The hut is accessed via a 26km journey that includes glacier travel, requiring both glacier skills and avalanche safety training. Sleeping up to 12 guests, it offers a remote and scenic base for ski tourers and mountaineers completing their traverse across the Wapta Icefield.

Distance: 20 km approach (from Bow Lake)
Elevation Gain: 820 m
Capacity: Sleeps 18 guests

Located on the Bow-Yoho Traverse, this hut sits along the glacier route over the Wapta Icefield. Sleeping up to 18 guests, it provides a critical shelter for ski tourers and mountaineers completing the traverse. Access requires glacier travel and avalanche safety training, with a 20 km approach from Bow Lake leading to this remote and scenic alpine base. The hut is open for winter use only.

name.en-US: Valley of the Ten Peaks (Neil Colgan) Hut

description.en-US:
Distance: 7km approach (from Moraine Lake)
Elevation Gain: 1067m
Capacity: Sleeps 18 guests

Located near Moraine Lake in Banff National Park, the Valley of the Ten Peaks (Neil Colgan) Hut requires climbing and mountaineering experience to access. The 7 km approach involves a significant elevation gain of 1067m. Booking a shuttle to Moraine Lake is recommended, though last-minute availability may be possible with a reservation. The hut sleeps up to 18 guests and offers a remote alpine base in the iconic Valley of the Ten Peaks.

Distance: 7km approach
Elevation Gain: 850m
Capacity: Sleeps 10 guests

Located in the Asulkan Valley above the Illecillewaet Campground, the Asulkan Hut is a popular summer hub for exploring high alpine terrain. In winter, it transforms into a snowy base perfect for ski touring adventures (Avalanche safety training required in the winter season). The 7 km approach with 850 m elevation gain leads to this cozy 10-guest hut nestled in stunning Glacier National Park.

Distance: 9km approach (from Asulkan parking)
Elevation Gain: 1375m
Capacity: Sleeps 4 guests

Located above the Asulkan Hut on the Asulkan Ridge, Sapphire Col Hut is a small bivy shelter ideal as a midway stop for alpine climbers and ski tourers. The 9 km approach with 1375 m elevation gain requires glacier travel and avalanche safety training. This cozy 4-guest hut offers a remote and strategic base for high alpine adventures in Glacier National Park.

Distance: 14km approach
Elevation Gain: 1425m
Capacity: Sleeps 8 guests

Nestled in a deep forested cirque in the Dawson Range of Rogers Pass, the Glacier Circle Cabin offers a remote alpine retreat. Access requires a challenging 14 km hike with 1425m elevation gain, demanding glacier travel and avalanche skills. The cozy cabin sleeps 8 guests and provides an excellent base for experienced mountaineers and ski tourers.

Distance: 12km approach
Elevation Gain: 250m
Capacity: Sleeps 8 guests

Located in Jasper National Park, Jacques Lake Hut is a family-friendly warden cabin open for bookings during the summer months. The hut offers an excellent base for ski outings near Jasper townsite and is accessed via a moderate 12 km hike with 250 m elevation gain. It comfortably sleeps 8 guests and is ideal for families and small groups

Distance: 13.1km approach
Elevation Gain: 985m
Capacity: Sleeps 6 guests

Located in Jasper National Park, the Mt. Colin Centennial Hut is a tiny summer-only hut used primarily by climbers attempting the rock routes on Mt. Colin and the neighbouring CR6 peaks. The 13.1 km approach with 985 m elevation gain leads to this remote 6-guest hut, offering a simple but strategic base for alpine climbing objectives.

Distance: 9km approach
Elevation Gain: 200m
Capacity: Sleeps 14 guests

To book the Family Rate, please call 403-678-3200 ext 0. Rates are set at the time of booking.

Located in Elk Pass, Kananaskis Provincial Park, Elk Lakes Cabin is a family-friendly hut open year-round. The 9 km hike with 200 m elevation gain leads to this cozy cabin, which sleeps up to 14 guests. It offers a comfortable base for hiking, snowshoeing, and exploring the beautiful Elk Lakes area in all seasons.

Distance: 8.5 km approach (from Gibson Lake Trailhead)
Elevation Gain: Moderate
Capacity: Sleeps 20 guests (summer) 15 (winter)

Located in Kokanee Glacier Provincial Park, this cabin is renowned for its winter ski week lottery and family-friendly summer access. In summer, it’s reached via an 8.5 km hike from the Gibson Lake Trailhead. The cabin offers comfortable accommodation in a stunning alpine setting and is only bookable online for the summer season.

Distance: 8km approach (from Woodbury Trailhead)
Elevation Gain: 800m
Capacity: Sleeps 8 guests

Located above the Kokanee Glacier Cabin in Kokanee Glacier Provincial Park, Woodbury Cabin is a summer-only hut accessible via an 8 km hike with 800 m elevation gain. Sleeping up to 8 guests, this cozy cabin offers a peaceful base for exploring the surrounding alpine terrain.

Distance: 7km approach (via Woodbury-Fletcher FSR)
Elevation Gain: 1000m
Capacity: Sleeps 10 guests

Located in Kokanee Glacier Provincial Park, Silver Spray Cabin is accessed via a challenging 7 km hike with 1000 m elevation gain. Sleeping up to 10 guests, this remote cabin is a destination for hikers and mountaineers looking to explore the more rugged and less-travelled areas of Kokanee.
